skill.name: frontend-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinates frontend implementation work across React, TypeScript, routing, state management, visual testing, and accessibility checks.
skill.summary: Guide frontend agents through component, state, route, test, and documentation workflows.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code formatting, and ordinary frontend terms. No executable script, command invocation, weak cryptographic use, system reconnaissance, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged backtick locations are Markdown code fences, inline skill names, JSON examples, YAML examples, and documentation references. They do not execute shell commands or instruct the agent to run arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are documentation blocks and inline Markdown references. No executable file or command execution primitive is present in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary words such as description, design, design_system, and desktop. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, cipher setup, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain frontend documentation text, not cryptographic code. The static pattern appears to match the substring 'des' in unrelated words.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Section Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance alert points to an anti-patterns heading in documentation. It does not collect host data, enumerate files, inspect environment variables, or instruct reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a documentation heading. Nearby lines list frontend anti-patterns and contain no host inspection or data collection steps.

content.value_statement: Frontend work often spans components, routes, state, tests, and documentation. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for coordinating UI implementation.
actual_capabilities: Defines a frontend orchestration role for React, TypeScript, UI, and UX tasks.
Structures context for component, route, state, standard, and test requirements.
Coordinates handoffs to specialized frontend skills for components, state, routes, styles, and tests.
Lists validation checks for rendering, form behavior, responsive layouts, keyboard use, and screen readers.
Connects frontend changes with backend API updates and developer documentation.
limitations: Provides workflow guidance only; it does not include executable automation.
References related skills and standards that must exist separately.
Does not define concrete React code templates or component implementations.
Requires project-specific context files to be useful in a real repository.

use_cases.title: Plan a Component Build
use_cases.description: Break a requested UI feature into component, state, route, style, and test tasks.

use_cases.title: Coordinate API-Driven UI Changes
use_cases.description: Track backend API updates and translate them into frontend loading, error, and state requirements.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Apply repeatable checks for accessibility, responsiveness, visual tests, and TypeScript correctness.

prompt_templates.title: Start a UI Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-orchestrator skill to plan a password reset form. Include components, state, route changes, and tests.

prompt_templates.title: Review Frontend Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-orchestrator skill to review the current component and state context for this feature before assigning work.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Backend Contract Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-orchestrator skill to translate this backend API update into frontend tasks, validation states, and documentation updates.

prompt_templates.title: Run Quality Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-orchestrator skill to audit a completed UI change for accessibility, visual tests, responsive behavior, performance, and TypeScript errors.

output: Identifies the form component, auth state dependencies, route placement, and reusable design system elements.
Assigns implementation, styling, route configuration, and visual test work to the appropriate frontend skills.
Defines validation checks for loading, error, success, mobile, keyboard, and screen reader states.

output_examples.input: A backend password reset endpoint changed its response format.
output: Maps the API change to frontend state updates, error handling, and loading behavior.
Broadcasts the frontend update status after tests pass.

output_examples.input: Check whether a new component is ready to merge.
output: Reviews TypeScript types, props, error boundaries, loading states, unit tests, visual tests, and accessibility requirements.
Reports any missing quality gate before merge.

best_practices: Provide current component, route, and state context before asking for orchestration.
Include backend contract details when the UI depends on API behavior.
Run accessibility, responsive, visual, and TypeScript checks before treating the task as complete.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill as a replacement for project-specific design standards.
Do not skip specialist implementation review after orchestration.
Do not accept visual changes without mobile, keyboard, and screen reader checks.

faq.question: Does this skill write React components?
faq.answer: It coordinates component work and quality checks. Actual component generation is delegated to a separate React component skill.

faq.question: Can it manage application state decisions?
faq.answer: It defines state context and rules. Project owners still choose the actual state library and architecture.

faq.question: Does it require Playwright?
faq.answer: The skill expects visual testing through Playwright, but the repository must provide the test setup.

faq.question: Is it useful without other frontend skills?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide planning and review. It is more useful when paired with specialized implementation skills.

faq.question: Does it include accessibility standards?
faq.answer: It requires WCAG AA validation and screen reader checks, but it does not include a full accessibility manual.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when the skill is available.
